chief_complaint: A 75-year-old male patient presented with a 3-day history of worsening cough, fever, and shortness of breath.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. He was not taking any medications. He had recently traveled to Argentina. He had not received any vaccinations in the past year.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, pulse 120 bpm, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg. Physical examination revealed bilateral lung crackles and decreased breath sounds.
labs: Chest X-ray showed bilateral infiltrates. Blood cultures grew Neisseria meningitidis. Complete Blood Count (CBC) showed leukocytosis with a left shift. C-reactive protein (CRP) was elevated at 120 mg/L.
